On views, same standard as the Global Social Dashboard so the two reconcile. Per platform: Google = YouTube public views (not the ~10M Google-network impressions); LinkedIn = impressions counted as views (no separate views metric); Meta = measured on-platform views only. Meta serves boosts across placements — the ~78% of Instagram-content boost that lands on Facebook / Audience Network is pure ads (off-platform leak), excluded here. Meta's ad export has no per-ad view count, so Meta views are measured at platform level (content-side), never estimated per ad. Scope is all ad spend; boost is standalone, never added to organic (anti-double-count rule).
